body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Description: Find all consumption sites of the changeDetection feature flag

# Search for references to changeDetection in the codebase
rg -n -C3 -P 'features\??\.\s*changeDetection' --type ts --type tsx
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 92

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Search for changeDetection references without problematic file type flags
rg -n -C3 'changeDetection' --type ts
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 4900

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Check the structure of common-preset.ts and how it defines features
cat -n code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts | head -250
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 10401

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Find where features object is consumed/used
rg -n 'features\?\.' code/ --type ts -A 2 | head -100
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 2983

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Get the full context around the feature flag definition
sed -n '530,560p' code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 928

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Search for TODO or FIXME related to changeDetection or agentic
rg -n -i 'todo|fixme|wip' code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts -B 5 -A 5
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 1476

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Search for "agentic" references that might provide context
rg -n 'agentic' --type ts
```

Repository: storybookjs/storybook

Length of output: 165

---



</details>

**Documentation claims `@default true` but no runtime default exists.**

The JSDoc indicates this flag defaults to `true`, but the default features preset at `code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts:205-220` does not include `changeDetection`. Since consumption sites use optional chaining (e.g., `features?.changeDetection`), an undefined value is treated as falsy, making the effective default `false`, not `true`.

This mismatch will mislead users who expect the feature to be enabled by default.

**Choose one of the following fixes:**

<details>
<summary>Option 1: Remove the misleading `@default true` comment (if feature should be opt-in)</summary>

```diff
 /**
  * Enable change detection for agentic UI review.
- *
- * `@default` true
  */
 changeDetection?: boolean;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Option 2: Add the default to the preset (if feature should default to enabled)</summary>

In `code/core/src/core-server/presets/common-preset.ts`, add:

```typescript
export const features: PresetProperty<'features'> = async (existing) => ({
  ...existing,
  argTypeTargetsV7: true,
  legacyDecoratorFileOrder: false,
  disallowImplicitActionsInRenderV8: true,
  viewport: true,
  highlight: true,
  controls: true,
  interactions: true,
  actions: true,
  backgrounds: true,
  outline: true,
  measure: true,
  sidebarOnboardingChecklist: true,
  componentsManifest: true,
+ changeDetection: true,
});
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@code/core/src/types/modules/core-common.ts` around lines 539 - 544, The JSDoc
for the optional flag changeDetection claims "@default true" but the features
preset does not set it, causing an effective default of false; to fix, update
the features PresetProperty<'features'> export (the features variable in the
common preset) to include changeDetection: true so runtime matches the
documented default, and verify the changeDetection?: boolean JSDoc remains
accurate.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:puma -->
